# Test basic server operations - StdioTransport, Accept, Close
# This tests the stdin/stdout transport and basic lifecycle

# Create a simple echo server to test
mkdir -p cmd/mcp-echo-server
cat > cmd/mcp-echo-server/main.go << 'EOF'
package main

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"flag"
	"log"
	"os"

	"github.com/tmc/mcp"
)

func main() {
	var name string
	var version string
	flag.StringVar(&name, "name", "", "Server name")
	flag.StringVar(&version, "version", "", "Server version")
	flag.Parse()

	// Test inferServerName when no name provided
	if name == "" {
		name = "mcp-echo-server" // simulating inferServerName
	}
	if version == "" {
		version = "(devel)" // simulating default version
	}

	server := mcp.NewServer(name, version)

	// Register a simple tool
	tool := mcp.Tool{
		Name:        "echo",
		Description: "Echo back the input",
		InputSchema: []byte(`{"type":"object","properties":{"message":{"type":"string"}}}`),
	}

	error := server.RegisterTool(tool, func(ctx context.Context, req mcp.CallToolRequest) (*mcp.CallToolResult, error) {
		var args map[string]interface{}
		if err := json.Unmarshal(req.Arguments, &args); err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		
		message, _ := args["message"].(string)
		return &mcp.CallToolResult{
			Content: []any{
				mcp.TextContent{
					Type: "text",
					Text: "Echo: " + message,
				},
			},
		}, nil
	})
	
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	// Use StdioTransport for the server
	transport := mcp.StdioTransport(os.Stdin, os.Stdout)
	
	// Serve using the transport
	if err := server.Serve(context.Background(), transport); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
}
EOF

# Test 1: Basic server with stdio transport
echo "Testing basic server with StdioTransport..."
mcptrace -- go run cmd/mcp-echo-server/main.go <<'STDIN' > server1.out
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1.0"}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/list","params":{},"id":2}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"Hello, MCP!"}},"id":3}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"_debug/exit","params":{},"id":4}
STDIN

cat server1.out

# Test 2: Server with name and version flags
echo "Testing server name/version inference..."
mcptrace -- go run cmd/mcp-echo-server/main.go --name custom-server --version 2.0.0 <<'STDIN' > server2.out
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1.0"}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"_debug/exit","params":{},"id":2}
STDIN

grep -q '"name":"custom-server"' *.mcp || echo "Server name test failed"
grep -q '"version":"2.0.0"' *.mcp || echo "Server version test failed"

# Check that server used StdioTransport and handled requests properly
grep -q '"result"' server1.out || echo "Server did not return results"
grep -q 'Echo: Hello, MCP!' server1.out || echo "Echo tool did not work"

echo "Basic server tests completed"
